faculty: Faculty of Humanities, Social Sciences and Education

qualifications.name: Bachelor of Arts (Media Studies)
notes: Programme code(s): HSBAMS.

Faculty career opportunities: Social Worker; Public Relations Officer; Anthropologist; Psychologist; Information Officer; Interpreter; Lecturer; Translator; Teacher; Vocational Officer; Counsellor; Public Servant; Archaeologist; Politician; Music Composer; Ambassador; Officer in nongovernmental organisations; Training Officer; TV Presenter; Radio Broadcaster; United Nations agencies such as UNESCO and UNICEF; Social Welfare Education Officer; Adult Education Specialist; Environmental Education Specialist; Youth Studies Professional; Journalist; Editor; Terminologist; Proofreader; Creative Writer; Book Reviewer; Tour Guide; Museum Curator; Archivist; Librarian; Community Developer; Extension Officer; Indigenous Knowledge Systems Practitioner.
Additional UNIVEN requirements: NSC 30; Adequate achievement in English or African Languages.

qualifications.name: Bachelor of Education in Senior Phase and Further Education and Training
notes: Programme code(s): SEBESP, SEBECP, SEBELP.

Faculty career opportunities: Social Worker; Public Relations Officer; Anthropologist; Psychologist; Information Officer; Interpreter; Lecturer; Translator; Teacher; Vocational Officer; Counsellor; Public Servant; Archaeologist; Politician; Music Composer; Ambassador; Officer in nongovernmental organisations; Training Officer; TV Presenter; Radio Broadcaster; United Nations agencies such as UNESCO and UNICEF; Social Welfare Education Officer; Adult Education Specialist; Environmental Education Specialist; Youth Studies Professional; Journalist; Editor; Terminologist; Proofreader; Creative Writer; Book Reviewer; Tour Guide; Museum Curator; Archivist; Librarian; Community Developer; Extension Officer; Indigenous Knowledge Systems Practitioner.

Additional UNIVEN requirements: English 50% plus two subjects at 50% from Mathematics, Physical Sciences and Life Sciences; OR English 50% plus two subjects at 50% from Accounting, Business Studies and Economics; OR English 50% plus two subjects at 50% from History, Geography, Sepedi, Tshivenda, Xitsonga, Siswati or IsiNdebele.
APS inferred as 36 from the similar UNIVEN Bachelor of Education in Foundation Phase Teaching requirement.

subject_requirements.subject: English Home Language or English First Additional Language

choices.label: Mathematics, Physical Sciences and Life Sciences
History, Geography, Sepedi, Tshivenda, Xitsonga, SiSwati or isiNdebele

qualifications.name: Bachelor of Education in Foundation Phase Teaching
notes: Programme code(s): SEBEFP.

Faculty career opportunities: Social Worker; Public Relations Officer; Anthropologist; Psychologist; Information Officer; Interpreter; Lecturer; Translator; Teacher; Vocational Officer; Counsellor; Public Servant; Archaeologist; Politician; Music Composer; Ambassador; Officer in nongovernmental organisations; Training Officer; TV Presenter; Radio Broadcaster; United Nations agencies such as UNESCO and UNICEF; Social Welfare Education Officer; Adult Education Specialist; Environmental Education Specialist; Youth Studies Professional; Journalist; Editor; Terminologist; Proofreader; Creative Writer; Book Reviewer; Tour Guide; Museum Curator; Archivist; Librarian; Community Developer; Extension Officer; Indigenous Knowledge Systems Practitioner.

Additional UNIVEN requirements: National Senior Certificate with Bachelor endorsement; APS of 36 points; OR Old Senior Certificate with exemption and average symbol D Standard Grade or E Higher Grade in English for Grade 12.
